project_reference.core_research_question: What are the explicit, machine-checked proof gaps in FALCON-512's EUF-CMA security reduction, and what do they imply for concrete security parameter selection?

scope.question: What are the explicit, machine-checked proof gaps in FALCON-512's EUF-CMA security reduction, and what do they imply for concrete security parameter selection?
in_scope: Write a 6-10 page manuscript centered on the Lean 4 formalization of the FALCON-512 EUF-CMA reduction and its 12 proved gaps.
Translate GAP 7 and GAP 10 into source-grounded concrete-security implications for parameter selection.
Use the completed Phase 1 claim audit as a framing constraint so the paper does not drift into unsupported hardware-to-attack rhetoric.
Produce the manuscript drafting, peer-review, and ePrint submission-preparation pipeline.
out_of_scope: Constructing a practical Falcon forgery attack.
Claiming that IBM Heron noise breaks deployed Falcon.
Running a new IBM hardware experiment.
Extending the Lean development into a full end-to-end proof of FN-DSA security.
Building a fresh cryptanalytic resource estimator from scratch.
unresolved_questions: Should the surrogate hardware-noise section stay in the main text or move to an appendix?
What exact title and abstract wording best balances the PQC audience and the Lean/Mathlib audience?

user_asserted_anchors: Primary contribution: Lean 4 formalization of 12 proof gaps in FALCON-512's EUF-CMA security reduction.
Critical findings: GAP 7 Renyi-to-TV square-root loss and GAP 10 ROM programming Q_H loss.
Main theorem: falcon_security_reduction with all error terms explicit.
Secondary contribution: supporting hardware-noise surrogate model with an explicit no-forgery-attack boundary.
Phase 1 claim audit is complete and must not be redone.
known_good_baselines: All stated Lean theorems compile without sorry.
Mathlib4 version is v4.28.0 in the existing artifacts.
The completed claim audit already narrowed the repository narrative away from deployed-system attack claims.
context_gaps: The exact manuscript placement of the supporting surrogate hardware-noise section is still unsettled.
The paper title and abstract have not yet been fixed.
crucial_inputs: User-stated deliverables: title and abstract, section outline, figure/table plan, first LaTeX draft, bibliography pass, theorem/notation consistency pass.
The existing Lean theorem names and error-term notation must stay recognizable in the manuscript.
The Grover/Heron benchmark is supporting context only and cannot become the paper headline.

formulations: Paper-first framing with the Lean proof-gap formalization as the main narrative.
Use the completed claim audit as a scope guardrail rather than as the paper's main result.
Treat the supporting Grover/Heron surrogate model as a cautionary toy-model stress test.
allowed_estimator_families: Exact theorem and error-term tracing from the Lean files into manuscript prose.
Concrete-security interpretation grounded in the gap report and Falcon-spec Renyi framing.
Section-level framing that distinguishes main formal results from supporting surrogate-model context.
forbidden_estimator_families: Exploit-first rhetoric that presents the supporting surrogate model as a real Falcon break.
Hardware-first framing that turns the 3-qubit Grover benchmark into the paper's main contribution.
Loose restatement of theorem bounds that drops explicit error terms or query factors.
allowed_fit_families: A manuscript structure built around theorem statement, gap taxonomy, concrete-security interpretation, supporting cautionary section, and conclusion.
forbidden_fit_families: A storyline that depends on the unsupported Grover-to-Falcon bridge.
Undated or overstated wording about deployment impact or standards status.
stop_and_rethink_conditions: If the title, abstract, or conclusion imply a deployed Falcon exploit, stop and rewrite.
If theorem names, gap counts, or concrete-security numbers drift from the Lean artifacts, stop for a consistency pass before continuing.
If the supporting hardware section starts to outweigh the main Lean contribution, re-scope the draft structure.

observables.name: Explicit EUF-CMA reduction bound
observables.definition: Machine-checked theorem falcon_security_reduction exposing Q_H * epsilon_SIS + delta_rej + delta_fp + delta_smooth + delta_hash + Q_S * delta_abort.

observables.name: Twelve machine-checked proof gaps
observables.definition: Complete severity-tagged taxonomy of the 12 proved gaps in the FALCON-512 security reduction.

observables.name: GAP 7 concrete loss
observables.definition: Naive Renyi-to-total-variation conversion degrades the 2^-64 scale to about 2^-32 in the stated formal path.

observables.name: GAP 10 hardness implication
observables.definition: The ROM Q_H loss implies an underlying NTRU-SIS hardness target of roughly 192 bits to support 128-bit forgery security in the stated model.

observables.name: Surrogate-model framing boundary
observables.definition: Whether the Grover/Heron and noisy-sampler results are presented strictly as supporting toy-model cautionary evidence rather than as deployed-system attack claims.

observables.name: Phase 2 manuscript package completeness
observables.definition: Whether the title, abstract, outline, figure/table plan, first LaTeX draft, bibliography pass, and theorem/notation consistency pass all exist and align.

observables.name: Publication readiness
observables.definition: Whether the manuscript has passed internal peer review and ePrint packaging checks.

claims.statement: The manuscript centers the machine-checked FALCON-512 EUF-CMA reduction theorem with all error terms explicit and faithful to the Lean sources.
The manuscript presents all 12 machine-checked proof gaps, with GAP 7 and GAP 10 highlighted as the dominant concrete-security findings for parameter selection.
The Grover/Heron benchmark and noisy-sampler Lean model appear only as supporting surrogate-model cautionary evidence and never as a deployed Falcon break claim.
Phase 2 produces a paper-ready first-draft package consisting of title, abstract, section outline, figure/table plan, LaTeX draft, bibliography pass, and theorem/notation consistency pass.
The manuscript pipeline concludes with a skeptical internal peer review and an ePrint-ready submission package.

deliverables.description: Working title and abstract for the formal verification manuscript.
must_contain: Explicit no-attack boundary for the supporting surrogate section

deliverables.description: Section-level outline for the 6-10 page manuscript.
Plan for the figures and tables that support the manuscript.
Table summarizing all 12 machine-checked proof gaps and their severity classes.
First full LaTeX manuscript draft for IACR ePrint.
must_contain: Concrete-security interpretation of GAP 7 and GAP 10

deliverables.description: Bibliography review and source-coverage notes for the manuscript.
Theorem-name and notation consistency checklist against the Lean sources.
Internal peer-review report generated by the GPD review panel.

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-main-theorem
acceptance_tests.procedure: Compare theorem statements, error terms, and notation in the manuscript against SecurityReduction.lean and ANALYSIS_REPORT.md.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: The manuscript reproduces falcon_security_reduction faithfully and any reader-facing paraphrase preserves the Lean mathematics exactly.

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-gap-taxonomy
acceptance_tests.procedure: Check that all 12 proved gaps appear in the manuscript package with severity grouping and source theorem names where relevant.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: All 12 gaps are represented and GAP 7 / GAP 10 are explicitly highlighted.

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-gap-taxonomy
acceptance_tests.procedure: Check that the manuscript states the naive 32-bit GAP 7 degradation and the roughly 192-bit hardness implication from GAP 10 without dropping the model caveats.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: The concrete-security discussion matches the Lean report and the Falcon-spec Renyi framing.

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-surrogate-boundary
acceptance_tests.procedure: Review the title, abstract, introduction, and conclusion for any deployed-attack claim or hardware-first framing.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: No section claims an actual Falcon forgery attack or uses the Heron benchmark as a direct cryptanalytic timeline anchor.

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-phase2-package
acceptance_tests.procedure: Check that all Phase 2 manuscript artifacts exist and reference one another consistently.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Title/abstract, outline, figure/table plan, main.tex, bibliography pass, and consistency pass are all present.

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-review-submission
acceptance_tests.procedure: Run the internal peer-review pass, then validate the build and packaging outputs for ePrint submission.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: A peer-review report exists and the submission archive validates cleanly.

references.why_it_matters: Primary summary of the 12 machine-checked proof gaps and their concrete-security interpretation.
Canonical Lean source for falcon_security_reduction, ReductionErrors, and falcon512_dominant_error.
Canonical Lean source for GAP 6, GAP 7, and GAP 8, including gap7_renyi_to_advantage_loss.
Canonical Lean source for GAP 9 through GAP 12, especially the ROM programming and forking-loss statements.
Supporting summary of the artificial noisy-sampler model and the explicit no-forgery-attack caveat.
Preserves the evidence taxonomy and the narrowed status of the hardware and timeline claims.
Locks the narrow framing constraint approved in Phase 1.
Source of the 3-qubit Grover benchmark and the measurements that motivate the supporting cautionary section only.
Primary scheme reference for FALCON sampling and Renyi-based security framing.

references.locator: Shi Bai et al., ASIACRYPT 2015, Improved security proofs in lattice-based cryptography: Using the Renyi divergence rather than the statistical distance
references.why_it_matters: Primary Renyi-divergence reference for explaining why GAP 7 is subtle and model-dependent.

references.locator: Thomas Prest, ASIACRYPT 2017, Sharper bounds in lattice-based cryptography using the Renyi divergence
references.why_it_matters: Primary Renyi-framing reference for the caveat that tighter Renyi handling can recover stronger security claims than a naive total-variation conversion.

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-phase2-package
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Centering the paper title or abstract on the 3-qubit Grover or Heron benchmark instead of the Lean proof-gap formalization.
forbidden_proxies.reason: The hardware result is supporting context, not the primary contribution.

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-surrogate-boundary
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Stating or implying that Heron noise makes deployed Falcon exploitable.
forbidden_proxies.reason: Phase 1 already established that the surrogate model is artificial and no forgery attack is constructed.

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-main-theorem
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Paraphrasing falcon_security_reduction in a way that drops explicit error terms or the Q_H and Q_S dependence.
forbidden_proxies.reason: The explicit error-term structure is the core formal contribution and must remain exact.

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-gap-taxonomy
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Highlighting only the critical gaps without explicitly accounting for all 12 machine-checked gaps.
forbidden_proxies.reason: The completeness of the gap taxonomy is part of the paper's value.

weakest_anchors: How much manuscript real estate the supporting surrogate hardware-noise section deserves without diluting the primary Lean contribution.
How best to translate GAP 7 and GAP 10 into paper-level concrete-security language without overstating deployment consequences.
unvalidated_assumptions: A 6-10 page format can carry the main theorem, all 12 gaps, and the supporting surrogate section without hiding key caveats.
The current Lean theorem names can be used as the manuscript's canonical naming layer with only light reader-facing explanation.
competing_explanations: The project could still read as a claim-audit note about hardware rhetoric rather than as a formal verification paper if the supporting sections dominate.
The work could be framed as a general formal-methods case study rather than as a concrete PQC contribution.
disconfirming_observations: If the draft's strongest claims still depend on the unsupported Grover-to-Falcon bridge, the paper framing is wrong and must be rewritten.
If theorem statements, gap counts, or concrete-security numbers drift from the Lean artifacts during drafting, the manuscript is not ready for peer review.

position.last_activity_desc: Completed quick task 001: manuscript summary, gap coverage check, and theorem-name audit

intermediate_results: `falcon_security_reduction` makes the EUF-CMA reduction error terms explicit.
The existing Lean artifacts identify 12 machine-checked proof gaps with GAP 7 and GAP 10 as the dominant concrete-security findings.
The supporting surrogate hardware-noise model is explicitly bounded to an artificial deployment scenario and does not construct a forgery attack.
Phase 1 fixed the narrative boundary: hardware and surrogate artifacts are supporting context, not the paper's main claim.
open_questions: Should the surrogate hardware-noise section stay in the main text or move to an appendix?
What exact title and abstract wording best balances the PQC and Lean / Mathlib audiences?
What is the cleanest reader-facing presentation of GAP 7 and GAP 10 without dropping the Renyi-divergence caveat?

decisions.summary: [Phase 1] Evidence tags are locked: measured, modeled, proved in surrogate model, unsupported.
[Phase 1] Primary sources win over storyline convenience.
[Phase 2] The manuscript is centered on the Lean proof-gap formalization rather than the Grover / Heron benchmark.
[Phase 2] The surrogate hardware-noise work remains supporting context only and must preserve the no-attack boundary.
Executed Phase 2 and produced the first manuscript draft package.
decisions.rationale: The title/abstract, outline, figure-table plan, LaTeX draft, gap table, bibliography pass, and theorem-consistency pass now exist and shift the next bottleneck to internal peer review.

decisions.summary: Quick task 001: draft summary and theorem-name audit
decisions.rationale: Ad-hoc review completed outside planned phases

pending_todos: Rotate the IBM Quantum token in the IBM account because it was previously embedded in the notebook and script.
blockers: The exact placement of the surrogate hardware-noise section is still unsettled.

session.stopped_at: Ready to start Phase 3 internal peer review on the new manuscript package
